summary: Manual capture of cap_creation-product-design-direction-2026-05-05.md (6813 bytes). 19 extracts found across 9 type patterns.

extracted.text: ## Decision: FAMtastic is a creation platform, not a website
extracted.rationale: ## Decision: FAMtastic is a creation platform, not a website. Layout must reflect that.

extracted.text: The single most important framing the user has given
extracted.rationale: The single most important framing the user has given. The current implementation treats the Workbench like a website (pages with persistent chrome around content). It should be treated like a creation product (canvas with collapsible chrome). Every layout decision flows from this. Reference: IDEs (Codex Desktop, VS Code), creation tools (Figma, Linear), generative AI tools (Leonardo, Midjourney, Cursor). The pets-assistant screenshot the user shared shows a collapsed-pane IDE pattern with header

extracted.text: ## Rule: When the user is actually creating, chrome collapses; canvas dominates
extracted.rationale: ## Rule: When the user is actually creating, chrome collapses; canvas dominates

extracted.text: When a user is in active creation mode (writing a prompt, editing an image, designing a component, e
extracted.rationale: When a user is in active creation mode (writing a prompt, editing an image, designing a component, editing a template), all chrome must collapse to maximum-information-minimum-space. The right rail slides away. Secondary nav collapses. The canvas dominates. When the user is browsing or selecting, chrome can expand. Mode is detected by user intent (clicked into a workspace vs. landed on a list).

extracted.text: ## Rule: Right rail is a glass slide-out, not a stuck panel
extracted.rationale: ## Rule: Right rail is a glass slide-out, not a stuck panel

extracted.text: The right-side panel must be a translucent glass slide-out, not a fixed boxy column
extracted.rationale: The right-side panel must be a translucent glass slide-out, not a fixed boxy column. It can hold tools OR information depending on context (the same surface, content varies). It slides in/out cleverly designed to show/hide. Sections inside must be thoughtfully grouped, not just a tool dump. Glass aesthetic is mandatory.

extracted.text: ## Rule: Secondary nav is a glass slide-out from the icon rail
extracted.rationale: ## Rule: Secondary nav is a glass slide-out from the icon rail

extracted.text: ## Decision: Page types each have their own layout pattern
extracted.rationale: ## Decision: Page types each have their own layout pattern. One-size-fits-all is wrong.

extracted.text: Different page types call for different layouts
extracted.rationale: Different page types call for different layouts. The previous plan defaulted to a one-template-everywhere approach. New rule: every page type gets a designed layout pattern matching its function. The ones identified so far (with their needed patterns):

extracted.text: ### Creation / Canvas page (applies to: Media Studio individual item, Component Studio editor, Templ
extracted.rationale:
### Creation / Canvas page (applies to: Media Studio individual item, Component Studio editor, Template editor)
- Canvas dominates the screen
- Tool/info panels are glass slide-outs from edges
- Prompt input (when applicable) is a glass panel that can collapse
- Results / previews need explicit thoughtful design — how many, what size, what comparison affordances
- Shay-Shay must have a defined location near the canvas so she can see the proofs and the canvas (not floating randomly)

extracted.text:
### Editor with chat (Sites > Template editor, individual page editors)
- Editor canvas + chat sideb
extracted.rationale:
### Editor with chat (Sites > Template editor, individual page editors)
- Editor canvas + chat sidebar
- Two distinct chats exist: Studio chat (per-page work) vs Shay-Shay (cross-cutting). Their relationship and surfaces must be designed.

extracted.text: ## Rule: Each domain gets its own coherent icon set
extracted.rationale: ## Rule: Each domain gets its own coherent icon set

extracted.text: ## Anti-pattern: Implementing layout before researching the page-type
extracted.rationale: ## Anti-pattern: Implementing layout before researching the page-type

extracted.text: The previous Ops plan went straight to "swimlanes + drawer + inspector" without asking "what kind of
extracted.rationale: The previous Ops plan went straight to "swimlanes + drawer + inspector" without asking "what kind of page is this and what layout does it need?" Anti-pattern: every new page must do design research first — what's the closest reference (IDE, creation tool, library, dashboard?), what affordances does it need, what tools does it need, where does Shay live?

extracted.text: ## Gap: No design research has been done for any of the canonical 7 domains
extracted.rationale: ## Gap: No design research has been done for any of the canonical 7 domains
## Decision: pause Workbench implementation. Research first, build second.

extracted.text: ## Rule: Every page-type design must cite at least 2 product references
extracted.rationale: ## Rule: Every page-type design must cite at least 2 product references

extracted.text: To prevent inventing layouts: every new page-type design must cite at least 2 product references (Cu
extracted.rationale: To prevent inventing layouts: every new page-type design must cite at least 2 product references (Cursor, Figma, Leonardo, Linear, Codex Desktop, VS Code, JSFiddle, etc.) showing the layout pattern is proven, what it gets right, and what we should adapt.
